title: Rank-average ensemble of distinct feature archetypes
summary: Blend rank-normalized predictions from genuinely different feature archetypes instead of treating duplicate submissions as independent evidence.
rationale: The feature search contains several distinct archetypes: current-tyre finish feasibility, fresh-slick option richness, and stop-debt or rule-state features. A single high-CV model may not be robust, while rank-averaging comparable but diverse archetypes can reduce variance and improve AUC if their errors differ.
implementation_hint: Train independent predictors on identical folds: a current-tyre finish-window feature set, a compact fresh-slick option-richness feature set, and the same compact set plus strict stop-debt features. Rank-average validation and test predictions, starting with weights such as 0.35, 0.45, and 0.20. Minimal first experiment: train two strongest distinct archetypes on the same validation split and average ranks 50/50. Tune weights only on internal CV, not public LB.
expected_effect: Small to medium potential ROC AUC improvement and lower fold variance if the models have comparable strength but different errors.
risk: Selecting archetypes based on public LB can overfit. Exact duplicate predictions must not be counted as independent models. Row order, folds, and leakage controls must match across all ensemble components.
